# truenas_user (Data Source)

Reads a TrueNAS user. Look up by `id` (Unix UID) or `username` (exactly one must be set).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up by Unix UID
data "truenas_user" "by_uid" {
  id = 1001
}

# Look up by username
data "truenas_user" "by_username" {
  username = "jdoe"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (Number) Unix UID of the user. Set this or `username` (not both).
- `username` (String) Login name of the user. Set this or `id` (not both).

### Read-Only

- `builtin` (Boolean) Whether this is an internal system user.
- `email` (String) Email address of the user.
- `full_name` (String) Full display name of the user.
- `group` (Number) Unix GID of the primary group.
- `groups` (Set of Number) Unix GIDs of supplementary groups.
- `home` (String) Home directory path.
- `home_create` (Boolean) Whether TrueNAS creates the home directory if missing.
- `home_mode` (String) Permissions of the home directory in octal notation.
- `immutable` (Boolean) Whether this user entry can be modified.
- `local` (Boolean) Whether this user is local to the TrueNAS server.
- `locked` (Boolean) Whether the account is locked.
- `password_disabled` (Boolean) Whether the account has no password.
- `roles` (Set of String) TrueNAS roles assigned to this user.
- `shell` (String) Login shell path.
- `sid` (String) Security Identifier (SID) for SMB-enabled users.
- `smb` (Boolean) Whether the user can authenticate to SMB shares.
- `ssh_password_enabled` (Boolean) Whether password authentication over SSH is allowed.
- `sshpubkey` (String) SSH public key(s) authorised for this user.
- `sudo_commands` (Set of String) Commands this user may execute with elevated privileges (password prompted).
- `sudo_commands_nopasswd` (Set of String) Commands this user may execute with elevated privileges (no password).
- `twofactor_auth_configured` (Boolean) Whether two-factor authentication is configured for this user.
- `userns_idmap` (Number) Subuid mapping for containers. `0` means `DIRECT`. Null means no mapping.
